annotations.label: Boundary-value problem

anchor.snippet: a boundary-value problem is a differential equation subjected to constraints called boundary conditions

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of boundary-value problem for differential equations; grep for 'boundary value' in Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: Solution to a boundary value problem

anchor.snippet: A solution to a boundary value problem is a solution to the differential equation which also satisfies the boundary conditions

annotations.note: No formalization of BVP solutions exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Well-posed boundary value problem

anchor.snippet: a boundary value problem should be well posed

annotations.note: Hadamard well-posedness for BVPs is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Boundary value vs initial value problem

anchor.snippet: A boundary value problem has conditions specified at the extremes

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes ODE initial-value problems via Picard–Lindelöf but has no concept of boundary-value problems for contrast.

anchor.snippet: A boundary value is a data value that corresponds to a minimum or maximum input, internal, or output value specified for a system or component

annotations.label: Temperature in an iron bar

anchor.snippet: Finding the temperature at all points of an iron bar with one end kept at absolute zero and the other end at the freezing point of water would be a boundary value problem

annotations.note: Heat equation BVPs are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: One-dimensional boundary value problem

anchor.snippet: Concretely, an example of a boundary value problem (in one spatial dimension) is

annotations.note: No formalization of 1D BVP examples in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dirichlet boundary condition

anchor.snippet: A type 1 boundary condition, Dirichlet boundary condition

annotations.note: Mathlib's 'Dirichlet' occurrences refer to Dirichlet characters/series, not PDE boundary conditions.

annotations.label: Neumann boundary condition

anchor.snippet: A type 2 boundary condition, Neumann boundary condition

annotations.note: Mathlib's 'Neumann' occurrences refer to von Neumann algebras, not PDE boundary conditions.

annotations.label: Robin boundary condition

anchor.snippet: A type 3 boundary condition is the Robin condition

annotations.note: Robin boundary conditions are absent from Mathlib (only unrelated category-theoretic 'Mor' results match).

annotations.label: Cauchy boundary condition

anchor.snippet: then it is a Cauchy boundary condition

annotations.note: No Cauchy boundary condition definition exists in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: A type 0 boundary condition has no physical boundary

annotations.label: Elliptic boundary value problem

anchor.snippet: For an elliptic operator , one discusses elliptic boundary value problems

annotations.note: Elliptic operators and elliptic BVPs are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic boundary value problem

anchor.snippet: For a hyperbolic operator , one discusses hyperbolic boundary value problems

annotations.note: Hyperbolic operators and hyperbolic BVPs are not formalized in Mathlib ('hyperbolic' there refers to trig/upper-half-plane).

annotations.label: Electric potential in electrostatics

anchor.snippet: a common problem is to find a function which describes the electric potential of a given region

annotations.note: Electrostatics and Laplace BVPs are not formalized in Mathlib.
